ASSESS / Standard move:
Wind was from 35-60º or so.  9-16kts.  Some upwind waves, but mostly shifty offshore conditions.  We said this would be a condition each side would win.  Wind was a little to the right of 90º to the beach, which seemed to help the right have more wind since there was more clear water that way.

We did a nice job of balancing when to go back to corner for more, and when to leave early and be prepared for an oscillation back the other way.

On upwind it seemed strong to be to windward approaching the corner so you could see the progression of the other boats and make your decision of when to leave that side.  When you are leading to the corner (the low boat) you don't always get to see how strong or weak you are on little shifts that would be good times to cross.

Downwind, gybe set was almost always strong but I was pretty cold and the boat handling made it hard to always trust in the gybe set, because I was pretty tangled at the top mark sometimes.

ASSESS / Standard move:
2 races in NNE breeze. 350-30º.  5-17kts.  Course 2 off point of land.
3rd race in filling breeze from W.  285º.  4-12kts.  Course 3 by C'an Pastilla.

NNE wind (course 2).  Was characterized by strong righties from Arenal and more gentle, but big, lefties from Palma.  Was a day to get to your side and then pick best lane back to middle.  
We gained by pushing more to corner as well as taking Tim's lane earlier.  It all depended on how the wind was looking more long term, and if it was sustained good to dig, if not maybe look to connect back to what was paying.  There were many shifts per beat, so not getting to lay line was usually good.  Downwind was open, but seemed good to just attack pressure.  Was not always easy to see.

W wind (course 3).  Similar to the final day of MSC regatta.  We wanted to look at the right but with a knowledge of shifts/pressure/finish top left.  Started middle of line and held in righty pressure, got nervous it wasn't going to come back, but in the end there was always something good about not being on STBD lay line.  Going straight downwind was also good.  Both in MSC regatta and this race.

We did a good job consolidating/protecting when we needed to and were unsure.

ASSESS / Standard move:
Northerly 8-15kts.  Race course 3 close to C'an Pastilla again.

We did a nice job of staying in phase but at least "touching" the best pressure.  Worked nicely for us to not always dig deep into pressure but rather get on the lifted tack ASAP because it was such a shifty day.  3-5 shifts per beat.

Downwind we still did a nice job attacking best pressure out of WW mark.  However, a few times we gybed early thinking we were sailing out of pressure when no one was really threatening us from behind and we can continue a little longer and close down the corner.  If bows are still pointing on our line behind us on downwind that is another indication there is not much rush.

We don't always need to gybe against the shift downwind (TP52 race Valencia example).  Sometimes best to just go get that header and worry about the lift back to the mark later, not usually a problem in the 49er.  Not as much as keelboats.
